title: pulassarium thyrsiflorum
intro: Pulassarium thyrsiflorum, also known as "Brahma's Bane" or "Kandarivalli", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Indian subcontinent, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, roots, and bark, which possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Pulassarium thyrsiflorum is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and relief from pain and discomfort. Herbal preparations of Pulassarium thyrsiflorum include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be prepared by steeping the plant's leaves, roots, or bark in hot water, or by boiling them in a mixture of water and other herbs.
